sections.paragraph:
ἀηδής, ές (ἀ-+ ἦδος ‘delight’) gen. οῦς (Hippocr., Hdt. et al.; also PCairZen 638, 11; Gen 48:17 and 1 Km 29:7 [both Sym.]; TestSol, Philo, Joseph.) unpleasant, odious. ὡς … μηδέν ἀηδές … ὑπομείναντος how (Justus) … had experienced no ill effects (despite drinking poison) Papias (2:9). Comp. ἀηδέστερος, α, ον more loathsome (of the illness that befell Judas and affected his genitals) Papias (3:2). S. adv. ἀηδῶς below.—DELG s.v. ἥδομαι.

gen. gen. = genitive

Hippocr Hippocr = Corpus Hippocraticum, V–IV b.c.—List 5

Hdt. Hdt. = Herodotus, V b.c.—List 5

et al. et al. = et alii (and others)

PCairZen PCairZen = Zenon Papyri, Catalogue général . . . du Musée du Caire—List 4

Sym. Sym. = Symmachus, Greek version of the OT—List 2, beg., 5

TestSol TestSol = Testament of Solomon, I–III a.d.—List 2

Philo Philo = P. of Alexandria, I b.c.–I a.d.—List 5

Joseph Joseph This abbr. used when no specific texts are cited (s. Jos.); I a.d.—List 5

Papias Papias , early Christian writer, II a.d.—List 1

Comp Comp date uncertain—List 5

adv. adv. = adverb, adverbially

DELG DELG = PChantraine, Dictionnaire étymologique de la langue grecque—List 6

s.v. s.v. = sub voce (under the word, look up the word)

Arndt, William ; Danker, Frederick W. ; Bauer, Walter: A Greek-English Lexicon of the New Testament and Other Early Christian Literature. 3rd ed. Chicago : University of Chicago Press, 2000, S. 23
